quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in mid-air by rapidly flapping their wings 50-80 times per second. The Anna's hummingbird, common in the Pacific Northwest, can even survive cold winters by lowering its body temperature at night to conserve energy.

short_read.title: The Secret Life of Garden Soil
short_read.teaser: There's a whole universe beneath your feet in the garden! Discover how a single teaspoon of healthy soil contains more living organisms than there are people on Earth.

medium_read.title: The Amazing Intelligence of Octopuses
medium_read.teaser: Scientists are discovering that octopuses can solve puzzles, recognize individual humans, and even display playfulness. These eight-armed wonders of the sea continue to surprise researchers with their problem-solving abilities.

long_read.title: The Budapest Coffee House Culture: A Golden Age
long_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian coffee houses were the heart of intellectual and cultural life in Budapest, where writers, artists, and thinkers gathered. This beautiful look back explores the elegant cafés that still serve as windows into Hungary's rich cultural heritage.

port_angeles.title: Gray Whales Feeding Near Port Angeles
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot gray whales feeding in the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Unlike most gray whales that migrate to Alaska, a small group spends the summer feeding in local waters, often visible from shore or on whale watching tours from the Port Angeles harbor.

puzzle_1.question: A gray whale can weigh up to ___ tons when fully grown.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right, Barbara! These gentle giants are truly impressive.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Gray whales can weigh up to 40 tons — about as much as six elephants!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Gray whales can actually weigh up to 40 tons! That's as heavy as about six elephants all together.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Gray whales make one of the longest migrations of any mammal, traveling up to 12,000 miles round trip from Mexico to Alaska. The ones you might see near Port Angeles are part of a special group that stays local all summer!
puzzle_1.learn_more_label: More about gray whales — NOAA Fisheries

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderfully done! Pillangó is such a lovely word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound like it's fluttering?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite unique in Europe. The word 'pillangó' may come from an old word meaning 'to flutter' or 'to fly lightly.'
puzzle_2.learn_more_label: About the Hungarian language — BBC Travel

puzzle_3.question: What national park can you see from Port Angeles on a clear day across the water?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! On clear days, the view of Vancouver Island across the Strait is absolutely beautiful. It's amazing to see another country right from your hometown!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The answer is Vancouver Island! Though not a national park itself, this beautiful Canadian island is clearly visible across the Strait of Juan de Fuca on sunny days.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is only about 15 miles wide at Port Angeles, so Vancouver Island feels close enough to touch on clear days. The strait was named after a Greek explorer who sailed for Spain in 1592.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: About the Strait — Olympic National Park

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
